contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मोवाच
शृणु वत्स प्रवक्ष्यामि आद्यास्तोत्रं महाफलम् ।
यः पठेत् सततं भक्त्या स एव विष्णुवल्लभः ॥ १ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmōvācha
śṛṇu vatsa pravakṣyāmi ādyāstōtraṃ mahāphalam ।
yaḥ paṭhēt satataṃ bhaktyā sa ēva viṣṇuvallabhaḥ ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Brahmā said — Verse 1: Listen, dear child — I shall proclaim the Ādyā Stotram, which yields great fruit. Whoever recites it constantly with devotion becomes beloved of Viṣṇu himself.

contentBlocks.original:
मृत्युर्व्याधिभयं तस्य नास्ति किञ्चित् कलौ युगे ।
अपुत्रा लभते पुत्रं त्रिपक्षं श्रवणं यदि ॥ २ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mṛtyurvyādhibhayaṃ tasya nāsti kiñchit kalau yugē ।
aputrā labhatē putraṃ tripakṣaṃ śravaṇaṃ yadi ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 2: For that person in the Kali age, there shall be no fear of death or disease whatsoever. The childless one obtains a son if one listens for three fortnights (forty-five days).

contentBlocks.original:
द्वौ मासौ बन्धनान्मुक्ति विप्रवक्त्रात् श्रुतं यदि ।
मृतवत्सा जीववत्सा षण्मासं श्रवणं यदि ॥ ३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dvau māsau bandhanānmukti vipravaktrāt śrutaṃ yadi ।
mṛtavatsā jīvavatsā ṣaṇmāsaṃ śravaṇaṃ yadi ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 3: If heard from the mouth of a brāhmaṇa for two months, release from bondage follows. She whose children have died becomes one whose children survive — if she listens for six months.

contentBlocks.original:
नौकायां सङ्कटे युद्धे पठनाज्जयमाप्नुयात् ।
लिखित्वा स्थापयेद्गेहे नाग्निचौरभयं क्वचित् ॥ ४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
naukāyāṃ saṅkaṭē yuddhē paṭhanājjayamāpnuyāt ।
likhitvā sthāpayēdgēhē nāgnichaurabhayaṃ kvachit ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 4: Reciting it on a boat in peril, or in battle — one attains victory. Having written it and installed it in one's home, there shall be no fear of fire or thieves at all.

contentBlocks.original:
राजस्थाने जयी नित्यं प्रसन्नाः सर्वदेवता ।
ॐ ह्रीम् ।
ब्रह्माणी ब्रह्मलोके च वैकुण्ठे सर्वमङ्गला ॥ ५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rājasthānē jayī nityaṃ prasannāḥ sarvadēvatā ।
ōṃ hrīm ।
brahmāṇī brahmalōkē cha vaikuṇṭhē sarvamaṅgaḻā ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 5: Before kings one is ever victorious; all the gods become pleased. Oṃ Hrīm — She is Brahmāṇī in Brahmalōka, Sarvamaṅgaḷā in Vaikuṇṭha.

contentBlocks.original:
इन्द्राणी अमरावत्यामम्बिका वरुणालये ।
यमालये कालरूपा कुबेरभवने शुभा ॥ ६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
indrāṇī amarāvatyāmambikā varuṇālayē ।
yamālayē kālarūpā kubērabhavanē śubhā ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 6: She is Indrāṇī in Amarāvatī (Indra's heaven), Ambikā in Varuṇa's realm, the form of Time (Kālarūpā) in Yama's abode, the auspicious Śubhā in Kubēra's mansion.

contentBlocks.original:
महानन्दाग्निकोणे च वायव्यां मृगवाहिनी ।
नैरृत्यां रक्तदन्ता च ऐशान्यां शूलधारिणी ॥ ७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahānandāgnikōṇē cha vāyavyāṃ mṛgavāhinī ।
nairṛtyāṃ raktadantā cha aiśānyāṃ śūladhāriṇī ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 7: She is Mahānandā in the southeast (fire-corner), the deer-vehicled Mṛgavāhinī in the northwest, Raktadantā (red-toothed) in the southwest, and the trident-bearing Śūladhāriṇī in the northeast.

contentBlocks.original:
पाताले वैष्णवीरूपा सिंहले देवमोहिनी ।
सुरसा च मणिद्विपे लङ्कायां भद्रकालिका ॥ ८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pātāḻē vaiṣṇavīrūpā siṃhalē dēvamōhinī ।
surasā cha maṇidvipē laṅkāyāṃ bhadrakāḻikā ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 8: In the netherworld (Pātāḷa) she is Vaiṣṇavī; in Siṃhala (Sri Lanka) she is Dēvamōhinī; in the Gem-Island (Maṇidvīpa) she is Surasā; in Laṅkā she is Bhadrakāḷikā.

contentBlocks.original:
रामेश्वरी सेतुबन्धे विमला पुरुषोत्तमे ।
विरजा औड्रदेशे च कामाक्ष्या नीलपर्वते ॥ ९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāmēśvarī sētubandhē vimalā puruṣōttamē ।
virajā auḍradēśē cha kāmākṣyā nīlaparvatē ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 9: She is Rāmēśvarī at Rāmēśvaram (the sacred causeway), Vimalā at Puruṣōttama (Jagannātha Purī), Virajā in the land of Oḍra (Odisha), and Kāmākṣī at the Blue Mountain (Nīlācala, Assam).

contentBlocks.original:
कालिका वङ्गदेशे च अयोध्यायां महेश्वरी ।
वाराणस्यामन्नपूर्णा गयाक्षेत्रे गयेश्वरी ॥ १० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāḻikā vaṅgadēśē cha ayōdhyāyāṃ mahēśvarī ।
vārāṇasyāmannapūrṇā gayākṣētrē gayēśvarī ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 10: She is Kāḷikā in Bengal; Mahēśvarī in Ayōdhyā; Annapūrṇā in Vārāṇasī; and Gayēśvarī at the Gayā pilgrimage site.

contentBlocks.original:
कुरुक्षेत्रे भद्रकाली व्रजे कात्यायनी परा ।
द्वारकायां महामाया मथुरायां महेश्वरी ॥ ११ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kurukṣētrē bhadrakāḻī vrajē kātyāyanī parā ।
dvārakāyāṃ mahāmāyā mathurāyāṃ mahēśvarī ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 11: She is Bhadrakāḷī at Kurukṣētra; the supreme Kātyāyanī at Vraja; Mahāmāyā at Dvārakā; Mahēśvarī at Mathurā.

contentBlocks.original:
क्षुधा त्वं सर्वभूतानां वेला त्वं सागरस्य च ।
नवमी शुक्लपक्षस्य कृष्णस्यैकादशी परा ॥ १२ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣudhā tvaṃ sarvabhūtānāṃ vēlā tvaṃ sāgarasya cha ।
navamī śuklapakṣasya kṛṣṇasyaikādaśī parā ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 12: You are the hunger of all beings; you are the shore and limit of the ocean. You are the Ninth (Navamī) of the bright fortnight and the supreme Eleventh (Ekādaśī) of the dark fortnight.

contentBlocks.original:
दक्षसा दुहिता देवी दक्षयज्ञविनाशिनी ।
रामस्य जानकी त्वं हि रावणध्वंसकारिणी ॥ १३ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dakṣasā duhitā dēvī dakṣayajñavināśinī ।
rāmasya jānakī tvaṃ hi rāvaṇadhvaṃsakāriṇī ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 13: You are the daughter of Dakṣa, the destroyer of Dakṣa's sacrifice; you are Rāma's Jānakī (Sītā), the very cause that brought about Rāvaṇa's destruction.

contentBlocks.original:
चण्डमुण्डवधे देवी रक्तबीजविनाशिनी ।
निशुम्भशुम्भमथिनी मधुकैटभघातिनी ॥ १४ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chaṇḍamuṇḍavadhē dēvī raktabījavināśinī ।
niśumbhaśumbhamathinī madhukaiṭabhaghātinī ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 14: O Goddess — you slew Chaṇḍa and Muṇḍa, you destroyed Raktabīja, you crushed Niśumbha and Śumbha, and you slew Madhu and Kaiṭabha.

contentBlocks.original:
विष्णुभक्तिप्रदा दुर्गा सुखदा मोक्षदा सदा ।
आद्यास्तवमिमं पुण्यं यः पठेत् सततं नरः ॥ १५ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viṣṇubhaktipradā durgā sukhadā mōkṣadā sadā ।
ādyāstavamimaṃ puṇyaṃ yaḥ paṭhēt satataṃ naraḥ ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 15 — Phalaśruti: Durgā — who bestows devotion to Viṣṇu, happiness, and liberation, always. The person who constantly recites this holy Ādyā Stotram —

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वज्वरभयं न स्यात् सर्वव्याधिविनाशनम् ।
कोटितीर्थफलं तस्य लभते नात्र संशयः ॥ १६ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvajvarabhayaṃ na syāt sarvavyādhivināśanam ।
kōṭitīrthaphalaṃ tasya labhatē nātra saṃśayaḥ ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Verse 16: — shall have no fear of any fever, and all diseases shall be destroyed for that person. They gain the merit of ten million pilgrimages — there is no doubt in this.

contentBlocks.original:
जया मे चाग्रतः पातु विजया पातु पृष्ठतः ।
नारायणी शीर्षदेशे सर्वाङ्गे सिंहवाहिनी ॥ १७ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jayā mē chāgrataḥ pātu vijayā pātu pṛṣṭhataḥ ।
nārāyaṇī śīrṣadēśē sarvāṅgē siṃhavāhinī ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Armor verse 1: May Jayā (Victory) protect me in front; may Vijayā (the Victorious) protect me from behind; may Nārāyaṇī protect my head; may the lion-vehicled one (Siṃhavāhinī) protect all my limbs.

contentBlocks.original:
शिवदूती उग्रचण्डा प्रत्यङ्गे परमेश्वरी ।
विशालाक्षी महामाया कौमारी शङ्खिनी शिवा ॥ १८ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śivadūtī ugrachaṇḍā pratyaṅgē paramēśvarī ।
viśālākṣī mahāmāyā kaumārī śaṅkhinī śivā ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Armor verse 2: Śivadūtī (Śiva's messenger), Ugrachaṇḍā (the fierce), Paramēśvarī at each limb; Viśālākṣī (wide-eyed), Mahāmāyā, Kaumārī, Śaṅkhinī, and the auspicious Śivā.

contentBlocks.original:
चक्रिणी जयदात्री च रणमत्ता रणप्रिया ।
दुर्गा जयन्ती काली च भद्रकाली महोदरी ॥ १९ ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chakriṇī jayadātrī cha raṇamattā raṇapriyā ।
durgā jayantī kāḻī cha bhadrakāḻī mahōdarī ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Armor verse 3: Chakriṇī (discus-bearer), Jayadātrī (giver of victory), the battle-frenzied (Raṇamattā), the battle-loving (Raṇapriyā); Durgā, Jayantī, Kāḷī, Bhadrakāḷī, Mahōdarī (the large-bellied).

contentBlocks.original:
नारसिंही च वाराही सिद्धिदात्री सुखप्रदा ।
भयङ्करी महारौद्री महाभयविनाशिनी ॥ २० ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārasiṃhī cha vārāhī siddhidātrī sukhapradā ।
bhayaṅkarī mahāraudrī mahābhayavināśinī ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Armor verse 4: Nārasiṃhī (Narasiṃha's power), Vārāhī (the boar-goddess), Siddhidātrī (granter of perfection), Sukhapradā (bestower of happiness), Bhayaṅkarī (the terrifying), Mahāraudrī (the greatly fierce), Mahābhayavināśinī (destroyer of all great terror).

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीब्रह्मयामले ब्रह्मनारदसंवादे श्री आद्या स्तोत्रम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrībrahmayāmalē brahmanāradasaṃvādē śrī ādyā stōtram ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus ends the Śrī Ādyā Stotram, from the Śrī Brahmayāmala, in the sacred dialogue between Brahmā and Nārada.
